items.title: West Brom sack head coach Ramsay after nine games
items.summary: West Bromwich Albion sack head coach Eric Ramsay after only nine games in charge at The Hawthorns.

items.title: WC host cities warn Congress of security concerns
items.summary: Local officials representing World Cup host cities warned Congress on Tuesday that security concerns, coordination problems and inadequate federal funding are risking catastrophe with little more than 100 days remaining before the games begin.

items.title: Wrexham overcome illness to cement playoff spot
items.summary: Wrexham manager Phil Parkinson revealed his side had been hit by a sickness bug in the lead-up to their hard-fought 2-1 win over Portsmouth.

items.title: Tiny Bodo/Glimt oust Inter Milan in UCL stunner
items.summary: Bodo/Glimt pulled off one of the biggest upsets in Champions League history by knocking out Italian giants Inter Milan on Tuesday.

items.title: PSG's Hakimi to stand trial over rape allegation
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain defender Achraf Hakimi insists he will "allow the truth to come out publicly" after learning he is to face trial for an alleged rape.

items.title: Snoop Dogg's surreal Swansea visit heralds late drama
items.summary: The difference between Swansea v Preston and other second-tier matches was that one of the Welsh club's co-owners happens to be a hip-hop legend.

items.title: 'Dit is de grootste stunt in de geschiedenis van de Champions League'
items.summary: De Noorse kranten zijn in extase na de sensationele uitschakeling van Internazionale door FK Bodø/Glimt in de Champions League . De stunt in San Siro leidde tot superlatieven, inclusief de vraag of dit de grootste stunt ooit is in het Noorse voetbal.

items.title: Infantino sure of 'spectacular' World Cup in Mexico despite violence
items.summary: BBC Sport explores the unfolding security situation in Mexico, as Fifa says it is "closely monitoring" developments before this summer's World Cup.

items.title: 'Bodø, een club uit een klein dorp in het noorden... Dat is toch ongelooflijk?'
items.summary: De complete bevolking van Bodø, dat zo'n 55.000 inwoners telt, zou in San Siro passen. Dat geeft nog maar eens aan dat Bodø/Glimt op bezoek bij Internazionale geschiedenis heeft geschreven. 'Kun je het geloven?'

items.title: Italiaanse media: 'Misschien komt er wel een film over het Bodø van Knutsen'
items.summary: FK Bodø/Glimt zorgde voor een sensatie in de Champions League, door af te rekenen met Internazionale. In het tweeluik in de tussenronde werd ook in de return in Milaan gewonnen: 1-2. De Italiaanse kranten spreken van een schok.

items.title: Sneijder spaart Inter na Europese dreun: 'Zeker niet beschamend'
items.summary: Wesley Sneijder vindt dat Bodø/Glimt de uitschakeling van Internazionale in de Champions League zelf heeft afgedwongen. In de ogen van de voormalige speler van de Italiaanse grootmacht is de organisatie van de Noorse stuntploeg van Champions League-niveau. 'De cijfers liegen niet.'

items.title: Champions League: Bodø/Glimt send Inter crashing out, Sørloth seals Atlético’s spot
items.summary: Norwegian side win 2-1 in Milan to seal giant-killing upset Atlético defeat Club Brugge; Bayer Leverkusen progress Bodø/Glimt dumped last season’s finalists Inter out of the Champions League with a remarkable 2-1 win at San Siro in their playoff second leg that sent the Norwegian minnows through to the last 16, 5-2 on aggregate. Under sustained pressure, the visitors struck in the 58th minute when Ole Didrik Blomberg seized on a loose pass on the edge of the Inter area and drove at goal. Yann Sommer pushed his shot away but Jens Petter Hauge reacted quickest to convert the rebound from close range. Continue reading...

items.title: Dundee United and Aberdeen play out goalless draw
items.summary: Dundee United and Aberdeen had to settle for a share of the spoils after a grim Scottish Premiership game with few chances.

items.title: Bodo eerste club buiten topvijf Europa met vier CL-zeges op rij
items.summary: Internazionale hoopte dinsdagavond de 3-1 uitnederlaag tegen FK Bodø/Glimt weg te poetsen, maar ging af als een gieter. Verder had Club Brugge op bezoek bij Atlético Madrid de kans om de achtste finale te halen. Lees de volledige avond terug in deze VI Live.

items.title: Newcastle finish off Qarabag in rapid time to set up Chelsea or Barcelona tie
items.summary: Eddie Howe adores motivational slogans and the Newcastle manager’s current favourite is: “One Brain.” The idea is to inspire his team to play with the sort of synchronicity that stems from a collective mindset and unity of purpose. For a while here it seemed to be working a treat with Newcastle’s intelligence – joint and individual – threatening to further humiliate Qarabag. But then, with a last-16 tie against either Barcelona or Chelsea assured, home concentration began, understandably, wandering a little. Commendably, the Azerbaijani title holders capitalised by fighting back; Gurban Gurbanov’s side, and, in particular, their Colombian forward Camilo Durán, showing they can play a bit too. Continue reading...

items.title: Papers: Man Utd join chase for Gibbs-White
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Wednesday's newspapers...

items.title: World Cup host cities ‘running out of time’ with $625m in funding held up by shutdown
items.summary: Funds promised for security have not been received Officials warn of potential cancellations to fan festivals Local and national officials expressed concern on Tuesday that the ongoing partial government shutdown in the United States could adversely affect planning and preparation for the 2026 World Cup, which is just over 100 days away. In a hearing before the House committee on homeland security, representatives from Miami, Kansas City and New Jersey – three locations that will host a combined total of 21 matches in the tournament, including the final – said they are still waiting on federal funds to be released to their respective local agencies. Last July, lawmakers pledged $625m in federal assistance toward World Cup security via the Trump administration’s “big beautiful” policy bill. Continue reading...

items.title: Williamson refuses to rule out strike action amid player overload concerns
items.summary: England captain concerned by relentless growth of game Ukraine World Cup qualifier set for Hill Dickinson Stadium England’s captain, Leah Williamson, has said she would never take the possibility of strike action off the table if players are not listened to over scheduling concerns. Speaking before England’s first two World Cup qualifiers, against Ukraine in Turkey and Iceland in Nottingham next week, Williamson said drastic action was “always a possibility” with talk of player overloading at the highest level a growing concern. Continue reading...

items.title: Swansea bow to co-owner Snoop Dogg with guard of honour before kick-off
items.summary: American rapper arrives to see game against Preston Snoop Dogg joined Swansea ownership group last July The Swansea City co-owner Snoop Dogg was greeted with twirling towels and a guard of honour on his first visit to the Welsh club. The American rapper, who is a minority owner of the Championship club alongside the television host Martha Stewart and Croatia international Luka Modric, made his first appearance at the Swansea.com Stadium for Tuesday’s clash with Preston. Snoop Dogg joined the Swansea ownership group last July and made his way to south-west Wales after being at the Winter Olympics, where he served as Team USA’s honorary coach as well as a special correspondent for broadcaster NBC. Continue reading...

items.title: Prestianni trains at Bernabeu despite match ban over racism allegation
items.summary: Benfica winger Gianluca Prestianni trained alongside his team-mates at Real Madrid's stadium on Wednesday, despite being provisionally banned for Wednesday's Champions League match between the sides.
